rationale: conn(phi) is a function on wffs (def:well-formed-formula) defined by recursion on formula structure (atomic / negation / binary cases); its well-definedness is exactly the structural-recursion principle (thm:structural-recursion). def:parse-tree is removed: the count is defined by structural recursion on the FORMULA, not via the tree -- and the parse tree (0116) is itself a sibling recursive construct resting on unique-decomposition, so it cannot be the basis here.
notes: Aligns with the house template def:formula-variable-set (0112), which has the identical recursive shape and correctly cites thm:structural-recursion. If the author instead intends conn as a parse-tree metric (internal-node count), the reviewer can restore def:parse-tree -- hence the moderate confidence.
